patches.hypothesis: The patch fix was overfitting as it was only handling test inputs and missing edge cases. The refined fix uses a more robust method to determine the instance type, adds more test cases to cover general cases and edge cases, and avoids the overfitting patterns identified. The refined fix ensures proper null checks and boundary conditions and fixes the actual root cause. The changes made include avoiding hardcoded values, handling general cases, and ensuring general correctness of the logic.
patches.original_hypothesis: The bug is caused by the `getInstanceType()` method trying to access the instance type of a non-constructor function type, which is not allowed.

patches.hypothesis: The error is caused by an invalid call to the `getInstanceType()` method on a `FunctionType` object. This method requires that the function type has an instance type, but in this case, the function type is null. The fix is to add a null check before calling `getInstanceType()`.

The bug is caused by the `FunctionType.getInstanceType()` method throwing an `IllegalStateException` when the function type does not have an instance type. The fix is to add a null check before calling this method.
